tipRules.tip: KVD: Post-frontal cold pre-spawn — work the Thunder Cricket like a jig. Get it down in the grass, hop it, twitch it. Let it hit bottom or grass, then twitch. 'Sometimes you got to make them react.' Bites are subtle — just gets heavy or the vibration goes away.
KVD: When wind is blowing and it's cloudy, fish are aggressive with a bigger strike zone — just wind the Thunder Cricket along. But calm/post-frontal = slow way down, work it like a jig.

knowledge.insight: KVD: Keeps 3/8, 1/2, and 5/8 oz Thunder Crickets on the deck during pre-spawn. Uses lighter weight (3/8oz) to slow down in tough conditions. Tungsten Thunder Cricket has smaller blade with tighter/subtler vibration and runs deeper in the same size — better for finicky fish.

KVD: In cold/post-frontal pre-spawn, don't just wind the Thunder Cricket. Work it like a jig — hop, twitch, pull-and-stop. Get it down in the grass and on the bottom. Bites are extremely subtle: the bait just gets heavy or the vibration stops. 'I always move faster than the fish' — force yourself to slow down.
